name: Briakinumab
description: Briakinumab is a human anti-IL-12 monoclonal antibody being developed for the treatment of a number of T-cell driven autoimmune diseases. It targets and neutralize interleukin-12 and interleukin-23, two proteins associated with inflammation, such as pro-inflammatory interleukins or tumor necrosis factor- alpha. Briakinumab represents a novel approach to treating psoriasis, Multiple Sclerosis, Crohn’s Disease and other autoimmune and inflammatory disorders. As of 2011 drug development for psoriasis has been discontinued in the U.S. and Europe.
indication: Investigated for use/treatment in autoimmune diseases, crohn's disease, multiple sclerosis, psoriasis and psoriatic disorders, and rheumatoid arthritis.
mechanismOfAction: Briakinumab targets and neutralizes interleukin-12 and interleukin-23.
pharmacodynamics: Briakinumab is a human anti-IL-12 monoclonal antibody being developed for the treatment of a number of T-cell driven autoimmune diseases. It targets and neutralizes interleukin-12 and interleukin-23, two proteins associated with inflammation.

interactions.name: Natalizumab
interactions.description: The risk or severity of adverse effects can be increased when Briakinumab is combined with Natalizumab.

interactions.name: Pimecrolimus
interactions.description: The risk or severity of adverse effects can be increased when Pimecrolimus is combined with Briakinumab.

interactions.name: Tofacitinib
interactions.description: Briakinumab may increase the immunosuppressive activities of Tofacitinib.

interactions.name: Tacrolimus
interactions.description: The risk or severity of adverse effects can be increased when Tacrolimus is combined with Briakinumab.

interactions.name: BCG vaccine
interactions.description: The therapeutic efficacy of BCG vaccine can be decreased when used in combination with Briakinumab.

interactions.name: Leflunomide
interactions.description: The risk or severity of adverse effects can be increased when Briakinumab is combined with Leflunomide.

interactions.name: Fingolimod
interactions.description: Briakinumab may increase the immunosuppressive activities of Fingolimod.

interactions.name: Roflumilast
interactions.description: Roflumilast may increase the immunosuppressive activities of Briakinumab.

interactions.name: Varicella Zoster Vaccine (Live/Attenuated)
interactions.description: The therapeutic efficacy of Varicella Zoster Vaccine (Live/Attenuated) can be decreased when used in combination with Briakinumab.
